MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-26-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kurt Putnam of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties William Simcic and Janet Simcic

RULING:
DEFENDANT J-M MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant J-M Manufacturing Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Simcic was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Simcic's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, Mr. Simcic's deposition testimony and declaration, the deposition testimony of James Reichert and defendant's responses to standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Simcic was exposed to asbestos-containing cement pipe attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to properly raise the issue whether exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Simcic's increased risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's responses to request for production (Exhibit 34) are sustained. Defendant's objections to Mr. Simcic's declaration and Mr. Reichert's deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted (as amended off the record): Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to CCP 36(a). Trial date is Jun-6-2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is June 9, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May-7-2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015, now included in Local Rule 20, shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is May 26, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the CCP, unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment / adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Plaintiffs' depositions must conclude no later than Feb-26-2016, if they haven't already. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than Apr-1-2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than Apr-15-2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by May 31, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on Jun-2-2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is May-20-2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is Jun-3-2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than Mar-4-2016. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than Feb-24-2016 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than Feb-24-2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. (Mini Minutes continued - see next entry.)
